response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with strong testing culture and open-source contributions, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming, whereas his C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project; his systems-level work was primarily in DLang. His large-scale infrastructure experience (high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) is relevant but relatively short, and he shows no formal technical-leadership experience over cloud/DevOps teams or ownership of a compute platform.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering experience across web, embedded, and desktop, with a strong TDD/craftsmanship culture
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and long-running open-source projects
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (roughly under 1 year professionally)
No formal technical-leadership role leading cloud platform strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
Career focus is web/product full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than cloud compute platforms or hardware/storage architecture

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. hard minimum
Limited cloud-platform and technical-leadership track record
